reasoning: The probability of a pilot report of a UFO from Africa occurring in the specific month of June 2025 and being posted on the NUFORC pilot index by July 1, 2025 is extraordinarily low. Our search shows that the specific NUFORC pilot reporting index `nuforc.org/subndx/?id=x` historically contains only 33 entries spanning 1966 to 2014, with exactly zero originating from the African continent. This indicates that pilot reports on this specific index are phenomenally rare (less than 1 globally per year on average). The geographic filter (Africa), the requirement for the reporter to be a pilot, the narrow 1-month sighting window (June 2025), and the extremely tight reporting deadline (must be posted to NUFORC by July 1st, despite their notorious batch-updating lag) stack together to make this outcome practically impossible outside of a highly coordinated anomalous event. There is no evidence of such an event or organized reporting campaign. I assign the minimum probability bound of 0.02.
